item.title: Trump achieved a historic victory in Iran. His deal risks squandering it.
item.description: His agreement is nothing like Obama’s, but it gives the regime an opening to recover.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s reckless vaccine policy backfires with flu outbreak
item.description: The Defense secretary is forcing society to relearn costly lessons of history.

item.title: Championship victories shouldn’t trigger destruction
item.description: Post readers on the Knicks, sports betting, camp and more.

item.title: Trump’s deal with Iran is awful. But what’s the alternative?
item.description: For once, the president’s lack of consistency and conviction proved an advantage.

item.title: What will you give an app access to?
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on app permissions.

item.title: This father of nine says: ‘Have more kids’
item.description: If you are worried that having children would be bad for the world, think about how much worse it would be if you don’t.

item.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal has a critical omission
item.description: The 14-point agreement does nothing for the U.S. prisoners left in Iran.

item.title: Voters can push back against unsavory politicians
item.description: Political parties keep asking voters to overlook scandals, bad judgment and unstable behavior because the other side is worse. Senate candidates Graham Platner and Ken Paxton are only the latest examples. Host Megan McArdle argues that character still matters in politics — and that voters are not helpless victims of polarization.

item.title: The surrender
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the war in Iran and Operation Epic Fury.

item.title: Here’s how to tell if Trump’s threadbare Iran deal is holding
item.description: The ceasefire ‘memorandum of understanding’ is vague, and Tehran knows the president’s choke point.

item.title: Removing civil servants can’t be as easy as Trump wants it to be
item.description: Civil-service reform and telework, in readers' eyes.

item.title: Trump’s big, beautiful brawl was the worst birthday present ever
item.description: A $60 million South Lawn cage match cheapened the People’s House.

item.title: The Democratic Socialists of America won’t be pacified
item.description: From Platner to Raman, a left-wing movement is poised to remake a national party in its own image.

item.title: Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ is a blockbuster argument against cynicism
item.description: The legendary director has made a film with the energy of youth and wisdom of a lifetime.

item.title: What retirement revealed about my father
item.description: I blamed him for what he couldn’t fix. Now I see he’s extraordinary.

item.title: The Hoover Dam let energy flow. Congress should take the hint.
item.description: The National Environmental Policy Act is the largest hurdle to energy prosperity.

item.title: The feral hogs ravaging America could come to your home next
item.description: Money alone won’t win the bipartisan war on feral hogs.

item.title: Remember Ebola?
item.description: The Congo outbreak is far from contained.
